name: syncthing-blade-mcp
title: Syncthing
description: Syncthing replication management MCP — multi-instance, token-efficient output, safe disk-space reclamation. Read and control access to Syncthing's REST API with a focus on replication awareness: knowing which folders are fully replicated across devices so you can confidently reclaim local disk space.
tagline: Mesh file sync with replication-aware disk reclamation
author: groupthink-dev

tools.name: syncthing_list_devices
tools.description: List all configured Syncthing devices with online/offline status. (DD-278 `scope=` tag accepted; folder set resolved via `SYNCTHING_<SCOPE>_FOLDERS` env var; canonical sort by deviceID; _meta envelope tail.)

tools.name: syncthing_device_completion
tools.description: Sync completion percentage for a device or folder.

tools.name: syncthing_connections
tools.description: Active peer connections — addresses, throughput, latency. (Track 2 — canonical sort on deviceID ascending.)

tools.name: syncthing_device_stats
tools.description: Per-device sync stats — last seen, files transferred.

tools.name: syncthing_pending_devices
tools.description: Devices waiting for acceptance.

tools.name: syncthing_pending_folders
tools.description: Folders offered by remote devices but not yet shared.

tools.name: syncthing_accept_device
tools.description: Accept a pending device. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_reject_device
tools.description: Reject a pending device. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_accept_folder
tools.description: Accept a pending folder offer. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_reject_folder
tools.description: Reject a pending folder offer. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_get_ignores
tools.description: Get the .stignore patterns for a folder.

tools.name: syncthing_set_ignores
tools.description: Replace the .stignore patterns for a folder. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_get_default_ignores
tools.description: Get the instance-wide default ignore patterns.

tools.name: syncthing_set_default_ignores
tools.description: Set the instance-wide default ignore patterns. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_folder_status
tools.description: Current sync state, error count, and need bytes for a folder. (DD-278 `scope=` tag accepted as folder-membership precondition; _meta envelope tail.)

tools.name: syncthing_folder_completion
tools.description: Completion percentage for a folder.

tools.name: syncthing_replication_report
tools.description: Cross-device replication summary for a folder. (DD-278 `scope=` tag accepted; folder set resolved via `SYNCTHING_<SCOPE>_FOLDERS` env var; sort tie-broken by folder id; _meta envelope tail.)

tools.name: syncthing_pause_folder
tools.description: Pause syncing for a folder. Reversible write.

tools.name: syncthing_resume_folder
tools.description: Resume syncing for a folder. Reversible write.

tools.name: syncthing_scan_folder
tools.description: Trigger a rescan of a folder. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_folder_errors
tools.description: Errors recorded for a folder.

tools.name: syncthing_browse_folder
tools.description: Browse files within a synced folder. fs walk — stable on disk.

tools.name: syncthing_file_info
tools.description: Metadata for a single file within a folder.

tools.name: syncthing_folder_need
tools.description: Files this device needs to be in sync.

tools.name: syncthing_remote_need
tools.description: Files a remote device needs from this device.

tools.name: syncthing_override_folder
tools.description: Override remote changes with local state for a send-only folder. Destructive.

tools.name: syncthing_revert_folder
tools.description: Revert local changes from remote state for a receive-only folder. Destructive.

tools.name: syncthing_list_instances
tools.description: List configured Syncthing instances (multi-instance support).

tools.name: syncthing_list_folders
tools.description: List all folders configured on an instance.

tools.name: syncthing_system_status
tools.description: System health — uptime, memory, goroutines, peers connected.

tools.name: syncthing_system_errors
tools.description: Recent system-level errors.

tools.name: syncthing_clear_errors
tools.description: Clear the system error list. Destructive.

tools.name: syncthing_system_log
tools.description: Recent system log entries.

tools.name: syncthing_recent_changes
tools.description: Recent file changes across all folders.

tools.name: syncthing_restart_required
tools.description: Whether a restart is needed to apply config changes.

tools.name: syncthing_restart
tools.description: Restart the Syncthing daemon. Write.

tools.name: syncthing_check_upgrade
tools.description: Check for available Syncthing upgrades.

tools.name: syncthing_health_summary
tools.description: Aggregate health summary across all folders + devices + system.

env.name: SYNCTHING_API_KEY
env.description: Syncthing REST API key (single-instance mode). Find in Actions → Settings → API Key in the Syncthing web GUI.

env.name: SYNCTHING_URL
env.description: Syncthing base URL (default: http://localhost:8384). Used in single-instance mode.

env.name: SYNCTHING_INSTANCES
env.description: JSON object for multi-instance mode (overrides API_KEY/URL). Shape: {"name":{"url":"...","apiKey":"..."}}.

setup.help_text: Find your API key in the Syncthing web GUI: Actions → Settings → API Key. For multi-instance mode, provide a JSON object mapping instance names to {url, apiKey} pairs.

fields.label: API Key
fields.placeholder: From Syncthing GUI → Actions → Settings → API Key

prerequisites: A running Syncthing instance (v2.x) with REST API enabled
setup.blurb: Syncthing replication management MCP — multi-instance, token-efficient output, safe disk-space reclamation.

highlights.text: Folder sync — list, status, completion, replication across the mesh
Reclaim disk space — safe-to-remove flags from replication analysis
Multi-instance — manage multiple Syncthing nodes from one server
Token-efficient — concise JSON by default, full detail on demand
Mesh-local — talks to Syncthing's REST API, no cloud egress
Device pairing — accept/reject pending devices, manage ignore patterns

readme:
### What it does

Syncthing Blade MCP gives your AI agent read and control access to one or more Syncthing nodes. Your agent can audit replication health across the mesh, identify which folders are fully replicated (and therefore safe to reclaim locally), inspect sync errors, pause and resume folders, scan on demand, and manage device pairing.

Ideal for fleet operators running Syncthing across a personal Mac fleet, NAS appliances, or a mix — where you want to know which copies are durable, which folders are stuck, and which devices are misbehaving without logging into each web GUI.

### Multi-instance from one server

Set `SYNCTHING_INSTANCES` to a JSON object and the blade connects to every node simultaneously. Every tool accepts an optional `instance` parameter; when only one instance is configured, the parameter can be omitted. Drop in a `tn-sb`, `viking`, `voyager` triple and ask the agent to compare folder coverage across all three in one call.

### Token efficiency

Syncthing's REST API returns large, deeply-nested objects (folder configs with per-device addresses, history, ignore patterns; system status with platform / version / connection counts). Each tool's concise formatter extracts the fields that matter — folder id, label, state, completion %, error count — keeping context windows lean. Pass `concise=false` to any tool when you need the full payload.

### Safety model

- **Reads** work immediately — folder listings, replication reports, device stats, system errors.
- **Mutations** are scoped to non-destructive operations — pause/resume folders, trigger rescans, accept/reject pending pairs, edit ignore patterns.
- **Override and revert** (send-only / receive-only conflict resolution) are present but operate on the folder's existing replication mode — they do not delete files outside of that mode's contract.
- **No folder or device creation** — the contract's `folder_add` / `device_add` / `folder_remove` / `device_remove` operations are deliberately not exposed in this blade; pairing is bootstrapped through Syncthing's own pending-device flow.

scenarios.label: Audit replication coverage across the fleet
scenarios.body: Pull a replication report from every configured instance, identify folders fully replicated to two or more peers, and surface the reclaimable bytes per host so the operator can decide which local copies are safe to drop.

scenarios.label: Reclaim disk space on a host
scenarios.body: Confirm a target folder is fully replicated on at least one healthy peer, pause the folder on the local host to halt deletion propagation, then guide the operator through removing the on-disk copy without breaking the mesh.

scenarios.label: Diagnose a stuck sync
scenarios.body: Read folder errors and recent system errors, list the files this folder still needs from peers, check per-device completion, and inspect recent file-change events to pinpoint where the sync is blocked.
